name: Fresh Apple Walnut Cake
recipeIngredient: 3 1/4 cups Apples; peeled, grated (about 5-6 apples, Granny Smith)
1 cup Sweet cider or apple juice
recipeInstructions:
1. Using an electric mixer, beat oil and sugar in a large bowl until thick and opaque.
2. Add eggs one at a time, beating well after each addition.
3. In a separate bowl, mix together flour, ground cloves, cinnamon, nutmeg, baking soda, and salt. Stir in whole wheat flour.
4. Gradually add the dry ingredients to the oil and egg mixture, mixing until just combined.
5. Add walnuts, grated apples, and applejack all at once, stirring until well mixed.
6. Pour batter into a greased 10-inch bundt cake pan.
7. Bake in a preheated oven set to 325°F for 1 hour and 15 minutes, or until a toothpick inserted in the center comes out clean.
8. Allow cake to cool for 10 minutes before unmolding.
9. Serve warm with Apple Cider Sauce: Melt butter in a saucepan, stir in brown sugar and granulated sugar. Add remaining ingredients, stirring until boiling. Reduce heat and cook for 5 minutes. Remove from heat and allow to cool slightly.
10. Drizzle the sauce over sliced cake, about 3 tablespoons per serving.
